full_name: Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs
blurb: Leads housing supply and affordability — residential tenancy, BC Housing programs, building codes — and supports local governments across B.C.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Tenants and landlords have fair, accessible tenancy processes
goals.description: The ministry funds the Residential Tenancy Branch, which sets and enforces the rules for renting and strata living in B.C. and resolves disputes between landlords and tenants.

main_services.name: Start, manage and end a residential tenancy

supporting.name: Learn your rights when starting a tenancy
supporting.desc: Information for tenants and landlords on tenancy agreements, deposits, and the rights and responsibilities that apply from the start of a tenancy.

legislation.full_name: Residential Tenancy Act  -  Tenancy agreements and starting a tenancy

supporting.name: Resolve a tenancy dispute with your landlord or tenant
supporting.desc: Apply for dispute resolution through the Residential Tenancy Branch — facilitation, a participatory hearing, an expedited hearing for urgent issues, or a direct request for straightforward claims like a missing deposit.

legislation.full_name: Residential Tenancy Act  -  Dispute resolution and arbitration
Manufactured Home Park Tenancy Act  -  Dispute resolution

mandate_tags.text: Support tenants with interventions that deliver affordability and a high quality of living without compromising rental housing supply or rental housing standards

supporting.name: End a tenancy or respond to an eviction notice
supporting.desc: Rules and options for ending a tenancy lawfully, including tenant notice, landlord eviction notices, mutual agreements, breaking a lease, and moving-out and deposit steps.

legislation.full_name: Residential Tenancy Act  -  Ending a tenancy and evictions

supporting.name: Check the rules on rent and rent increases
supporting.desc: Tenants and landlords can look up the maximum allowable rent increase, rules for paying rent, and calculators for rent-related questions.

legislation.full_name: Residential Tenancy Act  -  Rent and rent increases

mandate_tags.text: Support tenants with interventions that deliver affordability and a high quality of living without compromising rental housing supply or rental housing standards

main_services.name: Live in and manage strata housing

supporting.name: Understand your strata's bylaws and rules
supporting.desc: Strata owners, tenants and council members can learn how strata bylaws and rules work, including restrictions some stratas set on pets, short-term rentals or smoking.

legislation.full_name: Strata Property Act  -  Bylaws and rules

supporting.name: Resolve a strata property dispute
supporting.desc: Strata disputes can be resolved within the strata corporation, through the Civil Resolution Tribunal, in court, or by arbitration.

legislation.full_name: Strata Property Act  -  Dispute resolution

relationships.service: Resolve a low-value dispute through the Civil Resolution Tribunal

supporting.name: Wind up a strata corporation
supporting.desc: Strata corporations can terminate — wind up and cancel the strata plan — with an 80% vote of all owners.

legislation.full_name: Strata Property Act  -  Termination of a strata corporation

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Renters, homeowners and people in housing need can find and afford a place to live
goals.description: Through BC Housing, the ministry funds rent assistance, subsidized and affordable housing, homelessness services, and home-adaptation and home-ownership programs.

supporting.name: Apply for rental assistance as a senior renter
supporting.desc: The Shelter Aid for Elderly Renters (SAFER) program provides a monthly cash payment to help make rent affordable for B.C. renters aged 60 and over with low to moderate income.

supporting.name: Apply for rental assistance as a low-income working family
supporting.desc: The Rental Assistance Program (RAP) helps eligible low-income working families pay their monthly rent in the private market.

supporting.name: Apply for the Canada-BC Housing Benefit
supporting.desc: A joint federal-provincial rent supplement for low-income households, delivered by BC Housing alongside SAFER and RAP.

main_services.name: Find subsidized or affordable rental housing

supporting.name: Apply for subsidized housing
supporting.desc: Long-term subsidized housing for low-income B.C. residents who meet eligibility criteria, applied for through the BC Housing Registry.

channels.label: How to apply for rental housing

mandate_tags.text: Increase the supply of rental, co-op and non-market housing

supporting.name: Search housing listings for affordable and market rentals
supporting.desc: An online tool to search BC Housing's rental buildings and build a list of options based on your criteria, including affordable, market-rent and subsidized units.

main_services.name: Get help if you're experiencing homelessness

supporting.name: Find an emergency shelter near you
supporting.desc: An interactive map and list of emergency shelters, extreme-weather response spaces and drop-in centres across B.C.

supporting.name: Connect with a homeless outreach worker
supporting.desc: Outreach workers help people who are homeless or at risk of homelessness with immediate needs, and connect them with housing, income support and other services.
supporting.users: People experiencing or at risk of homelessness

mandate_tags.text: Support local governments and others affected by encampments with dignified, prompt and effective interventions to move people living in encampments inside before encampments can become entrenched

supporting.name: Apply for a home accessibility adaptation rebate
supporting.desc: The BC Rebate for Accessible Home Adaptations (BC RAHA) provides up to $20,000 to eligible low- and moderate-income households — homeowners, or tenants applying jointly with their landlord — to adapt a home for a senior or a person with a permanent disability.

supporting.name: Apply for the home owner grant to reduce your property tax
supporting.desc: The home owner grant reduces the amount of property tax homeowners pay each year on their principal residence.

mandate_tags.text: Work to make homeownership a reality for more British Columbians

supporting.name: Defer your property taxes as a senior or person with a disability
supporting.desc: Eligible homeowners — including those 55 and older, surviving spouses, people with disabilities, and families financially supporting a child — can apply to defer paying annual property tax on their home.

supporting.name: Appeal your property assessment
supporting.desc: Homeowners who disagree with their annual BC Assessment notice can file a Notice of Complaint with a Property Assessment Review Panel by January 31.

supporting.name: Buy an attainable home through a below-market housing initiative
supporting.desc: The Attainable Housing Initiative at the Heather Lands aims to provide homeownership for up to 2,600 middle-income households in Vancouver at below-market prices.

mandate_tags.text: Work to make homeownership a reality for more British Columbians

supporting.name: Access Indigenous housing programs and providers
supporting.desc: Information and provider listings for Indigenous housing on and off Nation, including Indigenous-run housing societies delivering rental and supportive housing.
supporting.users: Indigenous individuals and families, Indigenous housing providers

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Homes are built to code and short-term rentals support B.C.'s housing supply
goals.description: The ministry sets B.C.'s construction codes and builder licensing rules, and administers the province's short-term rental registry and enforcement program.

main_services.name: Build, renovate or buy a home to code

supporting.name: Check building code requirements for your project
supporting.desc: Look up the BC Building, Plumbing and Fire codes that apply to constructing, renovating or altering a building, including energy-efficiency and accessibility requirements.

channels.label: BC Building, Plumbing and Fire codes

legislation.full_name: Building Act  -  Provincial building code

supporting.name: Get a permit before you build or renovate
supporting.desc: Understand the land use and permitting process, and what building permits a local government may require for your construction or renovation project.

legislation.full_name: Building Act  -  Building permits and local government authority

mandate_tags.text: Work across ministries to expedite permits required for new housing construction

supporting.name: Become a licensed residential builder
supporting.desc: General contractors, developers and building envelope renovators must hold a residential builder licence from BC Housing and arrange home warranty insurance before starting construction on a new home.
supporting.users: General contractors, developers, building envelope renovators

channels.label: Apply to become a licensed residential builder

legislation.full_name: Homeowner Protection Act  -  Residential builder licensing

supporting.name: Register a new home for warranty protection
supporting.desc: Licensed residential builders must register new homes for third-party home warranty insurance before construction begins, and buyers can check a home's warranty coverage.

legislation.full_name: Homeowner Protection Act  -  New home warranty registration

supporting.name: Use standardized housing designs to speed up your build
supporting.desc: Pre-approved standardized housing designs and a Building Permit Hub help homebuilders and local governments move projects through design and permitting faster.

mandate_tags.text: Continue work with the Minister of Jobs and Economic Growth on standardized modular housing design

main_services.name: Register and operate a short-term rental

supporting.name: Register your short-term rental as a host
supporting.desc: Short-term rental hosts, platforms and strata hotel platforms operating in B.C. must register and pay annual registration fees through the provincial short-term rental registry, and renew each year to keep operating.

legislation.full_name: Short-Term Rental Accommodations Act  -  Provincial registration

relationships.service: Report a short-term rental compliance concern

supporting.name: Check if the principal residence requirement applies to your rental
supporting.desc: In many B.C. communities, short-term rentals are limited to a host's principal residence plus one secondary suite or accessory dwelling unit on the same property.

legislation.full_name: Short-Term Rental Accommodations Act  -  Principal residence requirement

supporting.name: Report a short-term rental compliance concern
supporting.desc: The Compliance and Enforcement Unit in the Short-Term Rental Branch investigates and enforces the provincial short-term rental rules.

legislation.full_name: Short-Term Rental Accommodations Act  -  Compliance and enforcement

relationships.service: Register your short-term rental as a host

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Local governments have the funding, tools and democratic processes to serve their communities
goals.description: The ministry funds local government infrastructure and operations, sets the legislative framework for municipalities and regional districts, and administers B.C.'s general local elections.

supporting.name: Apply for local government infrastructure or community grant funding
supporting.desc: Local governments can access conditional and unconditional provincial grants, including the Growing Communities Fund, infrastructure grants, and small community and regional district grants.

legislation.full_name: Local Government Grants Act  -  Grants to local governments

mandate_tags.text: Work with local governments to achieve housing targets and address concerns related to infrastructure

main_services.name: Take part in local government elections

supporting.name: Run for local government office
supporting.desc: Residents interested in running for mayor, councillor, regional district director, school trustee or other local office must meet residency and nomination requirements set by provincial legislation, and follow campaign financing and advertising rules.

channels.label: Candidate's Guide to Local Elections in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Local Government Act  -  Nomination and eligibility for local office
Local Elections Campaign Financing Act  -  Campaign financing rules for candidates

supporting.name: Vote in a local government election
supporting.desc: B.C. holds general local elections every four years on the third Saturday in October — the next is October 17, 2026. Eligible residents and property owners can vote at advance opportunities, on general voting day, or by mail ballot where offered.

legislation.full_name: Local Government Act  -  Voter eligibility and voting

main_services.name: Understand local government powers and bylaws

supporting.name: Look up your local government's bylaws
supporting.desc: Municipal councils and regional district boards make bylaws under provincial legislation covering things like zoning, business licensing and short-term rentals; check with your local government for the bylaws that apply to you.

legislation.full_name: Community Charter  -  Municipal bylaw-making authority
Local Government Act  -  Regional district bylaw-making authority

foundational_services.name: Ministry program and funding review
foundational_services.desc: Ongoing review of ministry programs and initiatives with the Ministry of Finance to keep spending efficient and focused on affordable housing, economic growth and affordability, in the context of provincial budget and cost pressures.

mandate_tags.text: Work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs programs and initiatives to ensure programs are efficient and remain relevant to delivering affordable housing, growing the economy and helping keep costs low for British Columbians

foundational_services.name: Housing supply and affordability policy development
foundational_services.desc: Policy work to increase the supply of rental, co-op and non-market housing, identify surplus government real estate for housing, remove barriers to small-scale multi-unit housing, and work with homebuilders on construction cost and interest-rate barriers.

mandate_tags.text: Increase the supply of rental, co-op and non-market housing. Identify under-used capital that can be leveraged to construct new purpose-built rental housing across the province

foundational_services.name: Housing targets and local government infrastructure coordination
foundational_services.desc: Coordinates with local governments to set and achieve housing targets and address the infrastructure needs that come with new housing growth.

mandate_tags.text: Work with local governments to achieve housing targets and address concerns related to infrastructure

mandate_legend.text: Work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs programs and initiatives to ensure programs are efficient and remain relevant to delivering affordable housing, growing the economy and helping keep costs low for British Columbians
Work to make homeownership a reality for more British Columbians

mandate_legend.text: Work across ministries to expedite permits required for new housing construction

mandate_legend.text: Support local governments, the Minister of Transportation and Transit, the Minister of Environment and Parks, and others affected by encampments with dignified, prompt and effective interventions to move people living in encampments inside before encampments can become entrenched
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Connect with a homeless outreach worker; Find an emergency shelter near you

mandate_legend.text: Work with local governments to achieve housing targets and address concerns related to infrastructure

mandate_legend.text: Continue work with the Minister of Jobs and Economic Growth on standardized modular housing design
